top_stories.title: SailPoint acquires AI agent security startup Entro for reported $200M
top_stories.summary: SailPoint is acquiring Entro Security, a startup specializing in securing AI agents and non-human identities, for a reported $200 million. The deal expands SailPoint's identity governance platform to address the growing challenge of managing and securing AI agent identities and machine credentials.

top_stories.title: Omada Agent Governance helps organizations manage AI agent access, risk, and compliance
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Agent Governance, a new solution designed to extend identity governance and administration (IGA) controls to AI agents and non-human identities. The product applies the same access governance discipline used for human identities to AI agents, addressing risk and compliance requirements for this emerging identity class.

top_stories.title: NewCore raises $66m to give AI agents a corporate identity
top_stories.summary: NewCore has emerged from stealth with $66 million in funding to build an identity governance platform for both human employees and AI agents. The platform addresses the challenge of managing and authenticating non-human identities, specifically AI agents accessing corporate systems.

top_stories.title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities | TechCrunch
top_stories.summary: NewCore has emerged from stealth with $66 million in funding to provide identity infrastructure for AI agents. The company focuses on giving AI agents verifiable identities as they increasingly operate autonomously within enterprise environments.

top_stories.title: Omada Agent Governance, Extends Identity Governance to AI Agents
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched a new product called Omada Agent Governance, designed to extend identity governance capabilities to AI agents. The solution provides organizations with tools to discover AI agents, establish accountability, understand access rights, and reduce risk across cloud environments.

news.title: Apple is bringing Hide My Email and Sign in with Apple under one domain
news.summary: Apple will unify the email domains for Sign in with Apple and iCloud+ Hide My Email under a single shared domain, private.icloud.com, later this summer. New email addresses generated by both services will use the new domain, affecting how Apple's privacy-focused authentication and email alias features are identified.

news.title: UK to require ID or face scan before you can make social media accounts
news.summary: The UK government will require age verification via ID upload or facial age scan for new social media account creation, taking effect spring 2027. Security experts have raised concerns about circumvention vulnerabilities and data breach risks associated with collecting identity verification data at scale.

news.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
news.summary: A China-linked espionage group compromised North American medical, academic, and military research networks by installing backdoors on REDCap research servers to steal login credentials. The attackers then abused Google Workspace email rules to silently copy and exfiltrate sensitive emails, maintaining persistence for over a year.

news.title: I Could've Rickrolled the FIFA World Cup. All I Needed Was My ID
news.summary: A security researcher discovered that FIFA's Agent Platform allowed open registration without proper access controls, exposing the Football Data Platform's Streaming Management panel to any registered user. The vulnerability granted access to RTMP ingest URLs and stream keys for all live FIFA World Cup 2026 camera feeds, illustrating a broken access control/authorization flaw in a high-profile platform.

news.title: AI demands an identity reset
news.summary: Computer Weekly published a buyer's guide examining how AI, particularly agentic AI, is transforming identity and access management systems. The piece includes interviews with early implementers of agentic AI and covers the evolving demands on IAM infrastructure.

news.title: Computer Weekly – 16 June 2026: AI demands an identity reset
news.summary: Computer Weekly's June 2026 issue features a buyer's guide examining how AI is transforming identity and access management systems. The issue includes interviews with early implementers of agentic AI discussing lessons learned for IAM infrastructure.

news.title: SimpleHelp bug lets hackers create rogue remote support accounts
news.summary: A vulnerability in SimpleHelp remote management software allows unauthenticated attackers to create privileged technician accounts on servers using the OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication protocol. The flaw enables privilege escalation through abuse of the OIDC authentication flow, bypassing normal account creation controls.

news.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
news.summary: Varonis Threat Labs discovered a chain of three vulnerabilities in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search, dubbed "SearchLeak," that could allow attackers to exfiltrate emails, calendar data, files, and MFA codes with a single click on a legitimate Microsoft domain link. The attack bypassed traditional anti-phishing and URL filtering tools because the malicious link pointed to a real microsoft.com domain.

news.title: The Onboarding Password Mistake That Creates Unnecessary Risk
news.summary: The article addresses security risks associated with temporary onboarding passwords, specifically how first-day credentials shared via email or SMS can persist beyond their intended use. The piece focuses on password hygiene and access management practices during employee onboarding, a core IAM workflow concern.

news.title: Assigning Microsoft Graph API Application Permissions to a Managed Identity Using Azure CLI
news.summary: The article covers how to assign Microsoft Graph API application permissions to Azure Managed Identities using Azure CLI. It includes technical guidance on retrieving App Role IDs and the Microsoft Entra roles required to perform the assignment.

news.title: Lumos Launches the Identity Agent Force to Govern Access for Every Human, NHI, and AI Identity
news.summary: Lumos has launched the "Identity Agent Force," a set of AI-powered capabilities designed to automate identity governance across human, non-human, and AI agent identities. The product targets identity teams managing NHI and agentic AI access, addressing the growing challenge of continuous access governance at scale.

news.title: NewCore raises $66M to build identity infrastructure for AI agents
news.summary: NewCore has raised $66M to develop identity infrastructure specifically designed for AI agents. The funding targets non-human identity management, addressing the growing need to authenticate and authorize AI agents operating within enterprise environments.

news.title: Keeper Security Introduces Universal Secrets Sync to Eliminate Credential Drift Across Cloud Environments
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched Universal Secrets Sync, a new KeeperPAM feature that automatically distributes rotated secrets across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud in a single rotation event. The capability is designed to eliminate credential drift by removing manual steps from the secrets rotation process across multi-cloud environments.

news.title: Why your help desk is still your biggest security risk
news.summary: The article addresses help desk operations as a critical security vulnerability in identity management, particularly as AI amplifies risks. The focus is on identity verification weaknesses at the help desk level, a known attack vector used in social engineering attacks to bypass authentication controls.

news.title: AI agents are reshaping identity governance, and attackers are already exploiting the gap
news.summary: AI agents are creating new challenges for identity governance as autonomous systems require access permissions that traditional IAM frameworks were not designed to handle. Attackers are actively exploiting the gap between existing identity governance models and the access management requirements of AI agents in enterprise environments.

news.title: Securing AI agents in Microsoft Entra with Conditional Access
news.summary: Microsoft is updating its identity security framework in Microsoft Entra to address security risks posed by autonomous AI agents. The update extends Conditional Access policies to cover non-human AI agent identities, as traditional MFA controls are deemed insufficient for entities operating at machine speed and scale.

news.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
news.summary: A China-linked threat group tracked as Velvet Ant backdoored Linux PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) and OpenSSH components to maintain persistent access inside targeted networks for nearly a decade. The group was discovered by Sygnia, which found the attackers had embedded themselves within the authentication layer itself, allowing them to survive standard remediation efforts.

news.title: Linx Security Integrates with Claude Compliance API to Bring Identity Governance to Enterprise AI
news.summary: Linx Security announced an integration between its identity security and governance platform and Claude's Compliance API. The integration is designed to extend identity governance capabilities to enterprise AI environments, covering human, AI agent, and non-human identities.

news.title: Microsoft Entra adds device soft-delete and cloud-native sync migration
news.summary: Microsoft Entra has introduced a soft-delete feature for device objects in public preview, enabling administrators to recover accidentally deleted Entra joined or registered devices within a retention period. The update also includes a cloud-native sync migration capability, with the soft-delete mechanism preserving critical identity and device registration data during the recovery window.

news.title: Levi & Korsinsky Announces Investigation of Securities Claims Against SailPoint, Inc. (SAIL)
news.summary: Law firm Levi & Korsinsky has announced a securities investigation into SailPoint, Inc. following a significant stock drop of approximately 12% after the company disclosed a revised forward outlook on June 9, 2026. The investigation centers on whether SailPoint misled investors about its growth trajectory prior to that disclosure.

news.title: Teleport Named Most Innovative Identity and Access Management Platform in 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards
news.summary: Teleport has been named the most innovative IAM platform in The Hacker News 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards. The recognition is based on Teleport's infrastructure identity architecture, which focuses on resiliency, scalability, and agility for modern computing environments.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including those of the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force's Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal account recovery processes were circulated on Telegram, resulting in temporary account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries from both houses. CISA is actively working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed credentials to multiple highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files contained details about CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.
